task: I’m putting together a super short CapCut quick-start note for a friend who edits on a Mac and is totally new to CapCut Desktop, so could you help me pull the pieces together in a way that follows a real beginner workflow? Start on Google and find a beginner-friendly source that shows how to make a plain black screen clip in CapCut Desktop, then open the actual result page and keep it open so I can see the instructions myself. After that, still using Google, find a clear source that explains how to reorder or move clips around in the CapCut timeline, because I want the note to explain how to drop that black screen into the right spot in a project; open that source in its own tab too so I can compare the two instructions side by side. Then go to CapCut’s own website and find their instructions for adding curved text, since I want one slightly more advanced text trick in the same note, and leave the CapCut page open as proof of the official steps. Finally, go to Apple Support and find Apple’s official instructions for calibrating a Mac display with Display Calibrator Assistant, because if the screen is off then the black screen and text styling can look wrong; open the Apple page and pull out the key steps for actually running the assistant. When you’re done, give me one compact how-to note that ties these together as a simple workflow: make the black screen, move it into place on the timeline, add curved text if needed, and then calibrate the Mac display if colors or contrast look off.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully searched for and opened a beginner-friendly source for creating a black screen in CapCut Desktop (a YouTube video titled "CapCut PC: How to Insert a Black Screen"). The agent verified the content of this source via transcript analysis and included a summary of the instructions in the final note provided to the user. The source remained open as requested.

R2: success - The agent successfully searched for and opened a YouTube video titled "How to change order of clips in CapCut (Easiest Way)(2026 Guide)" (Step 9). The agent then summarized the process of moving clips in the final note provided in Step 30, specifically stating: "Find the black background clip in the timeline. Click and hold it, then drag it left or right where it belongs. Drop it on the main video track, not an overlay/audio/text layer." This satisfies the requirement to find a source, open it in a tab, and provide a summary for positioning the black screen.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official CapCut website (https://www.capcut.com/resource/create-curved-text) to find instructions for adding curved text. The agent kept this tab open throughout the process and included a summary in the final note that explicitly attributes the curved text process to CapCut's official site.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Apple Support page "Calibrate your Mac display" (https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/calibrate-your-display-mchlp1109/mac). In the final response, the agent provided a summary of the key steps to run the Display Calibrator Assistant: navigating to System Settings > Displays, selecting the Color profile menu, choosing Customize, clicking the Add button, and following the assistant's prompts. This accurately reflects the process for running the assistant on a Mac.

R5: success - The agent successfully gathered all four required sources (black screen, clip reordering, curved text, and Mac display calibration) and kept them open in tabs. It then synthesized this information into a single, compact, and logical workflow note as requested. The note integrates the steps into a cohesive sequence (make black screen -> move it -> add curved text -> calibrate display if needed), satisfying the requirement for a single simple editing workflow.
